description: Arabic-Hebrew False Friend semantic disambiguation with ABC-randomized multiple choice. C is a trap option (never correct).

definitions.A: The Arabic sentence is semantically appropriate (Hebrew sentence uses false friend with wrong meaning).
definitions.B: The Hebrew sentence is semantically appropriate (Arabic sentence uses false friend with wrong meaning).
definitions.C: Both sentences are semantically appropriate (TRAP - never correct for false friends).

undiac.name: False Friend Disambiguation / undiac

diac.name: False Friend Disambiguation / diac

uroman.name: False Friend Disambiguation / uroman

ipa.name: False Friend Disambiguation / IPA

undiac_uroman.name: False Friend Disambiguation / undiac+uroman
undiac_uroman.template:
You are given two sentences: one in Arabic script and one in Hebrew script.
Each sentence is also provided in Uroman transliteration.

Which sentence is semantically appropriate?

A. {sent_a}
B. {sent_b}
C. Both are appropriate

Answer with only the letter (A/B/C).

undiac_ipa.name: False Friend Disambiguation / undiac+IPA
undiac_ipa.template:
You are given two sentences: one in Arabic script and one in Hebrew script.
Each sentence is also provided in IPA transcription.

Which sentence is semantically appropriate?

A. {sent_a}
B. {sent_b}
C. Both are appropriate

Answer with only the letter (A/B/C).
